brands.name: Fresh Monkee
brands.notes: Connecticut-based made-to-order protein shake, smoothie and bowl franchise (DBA 'The Fresh Monkee'). Emerging but actively growing system; began franchising 2022 with ~39 stores across 17 states as of early 2026.

riskFlags.title: Young franchise system
riskFlags.detail: Founded 2014 but only began franchising in 2022, so the franchised track record is short (~4 years).

nationwide.note: Single and multi-unit franchises offered nationally in key US markets; registration states listed include AL, AZ, AR, CO, CT, DE, FL, GA, IA, ID, IN, KS, KY, LA, MA, ME, MI, MO, MS, MT, NC, ND, NE, NH, NJ, NM, NV, NY, OH, OK, OR, PA and others.

liquidCapitalMin.note: At least $75,000 in liquid assets for a single unit.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Fresh Monkee official franchise site / Sharpsheets
totalInvestmentMin.note: Single-unit range $181,200-$380,300 incl. construction, equipment, 3 months working capital and franchise fee.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Fresh Monkee official franchise site / Sharpsheets

avgUnitRevenue.note: Reported AUV of ~$595,000 per Item 19; represents average annual revenue of franchised units, not profit.

totalUnits.note: ~39 stores in 17 states as of Jan 2026 (31 reported Dec 2025).

yearFounded.note: Founded by Judy Flynn in Wethersfield, CT.

brands.name: FunBox
brands.notes: Large traveling/pop-up inflatable amusement park franchise (giant bounce attractions). Emerging high-investment recreation concept that began franchising around 2021-2022; unit counts reported inconsistently across sources (~30-40).

riskFlags.title: High capital requirement for an emerging brand
riskFlags.detail: Total investment is very high ($665K-$1.5M+) for a young recreation franchise with limited operating history and a seasonal/event-driven revenue model.

riskFlags.title: Inconsistent / sparse public data
riskFlags.detail: Reported founding year (2019 vs 2022) and unit counts (30-40) vary across directories; Entrepreneur directory shows 'No data available'. Franchise details gated behind direct contact on official site.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Reported range $665,000-$1,505,000; some sources cite up to ~$1.63M. Varies by indoor/outdoor and lease vs purchase.

totalUnits.note: Reported ~37 units; other sources cite 30-40. Treat as approximate.

trainingWeeks.note: Approx. 34 total training hours (27 on-the-job + 7 classroom), i.e. roughly one week.

brands.name: Fuse Service
brands.notes: Home-services franchise (HVAC, plumbing, electrical and appliance repair), also marketed as Fuse Service HVAC & Appliance Repair. Began franchising in 2021 and reports 30-54 locations across the US; emerging low-to-mid investment system with a tiered royalty.

riskFlags.title: Young franchise system with varying unit reporting
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only since 2021; official site cites '30+ franchisees' while directory data cites up to ~54 locations, indicating fast but recently-established growth and limited long-term track record.
riskFlags.source: Fuse Center official site / Entrepreneur directory

nationwide.source: Fuse Service / Fuse Center official site
nationwide.note: Markets itself as operating franchise locations nationwide across the USA ('30+ successful franchisees across the USA').

royaltyPercent.source: Fuse Center franchise royalty fees page
royaltyPercent.note: Tiered/declining royalty: 3% of gross sales up to $499,999.99; 2.5% $500K-$999,999.99; 2% $1M-$1,999,999.99; 1.5% at $2M+. Headline is the entry (lowest-tier sales band) rate.

totalUnits.note: Reported 'more than 54' locations; official site cites 30+. Treat as approximate.

canRunFromHome.note: Mobile/dispatch home-services model; low buildout footprint implied. Confidence low pending FDD confirmation.

brands.name: Garage Kings
brands.notes: Garage floor coating and storage/organization franchise. Emerging system (~32 locations incl. company-owned at end of 2023) with a published Item 19 showing a notable gap between affiliate-owned and franchised unit revenue.

riskFlags.title: Affiliate vs franchised revenue gap in Item 19
riskFlags.detail: 2023 FDD Item 19 shows affiliate-owned locations averaging ~$1.04M while franchised locations averaged ~$628,601 - a material gap prospective franchisees should weigh.

riskFlags.title: Non-exclusive territories
riskFlags.detail: Territory is defined by zip codes (~100,000 single-family homes) but is NOT exclusive; franchisees may face competition from other franchisees or company-owned outlets.

riskFlags.title: Not ranked on Entrepreneur Franchise 500
riskFlags.detail: Garage Kings did not rank on Entrepreneur's 2025 Franchise 500 list, reflecting limited mainstream scale/recognition.

adFundPercent.note: 2% of weekly revenue Brand Development Fund (after initial $1,000); separate local marketing min of greater of $48K/yr or 5%.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19 average annual revenue for FRANCHISED locations was $628,601 in 2023 (affiliate-owned averaged $1,042,489). Represents revenue, not profit.

trainingWeeks.note: $7,500 training fee plus $5,300-$10,000 additional training expenses; duration approximate.

exclusiveTerritories.note: Territories are zip-code defined but non-exclusive.

brands.name: Graze Craze
brands.notes: Charcuterie board and grazing-table food franchise owned by United Franchise Group (UFG) since 2021. Despite early-FDD-era controversy, the brand is currently growing - it opened its 100th location in Oct 2025 and reported 33% same-store sales growth in 2024.

riskFlags.title: Franchisor operated at a loss with negative equity (FY2024)
riskFlags.detail: Graze Craze Franchising, LLC reported a net loss of $1,473,563 for FY ended 6/30/2024 (and $1,305,551 the prior year) with negative member's equity (deficit) of $2,777,067 as of 6/30/2024 - a financial-health red flag for the franchisor entity.
riskFlags.source: Unhappy Franchisee (citing Graze Craze FDD audited financials)

riskFlags.title: Disclosure / management-history concerns raised in early FDDs
riskFlags.detail: Critics flagged the 2021-2022 FDDs for a non-disclosed affiliate operator's background and an early-stage concept with limited track record. UFG-related entities have also been subject to a California DFPI consent order (UFG family). Weigh against the brand's subsequent growth.

riskFlags.title: United Franchise Group portfolio brand
riskFlags.detail: Graze Craze is one of many UFG concepts (food division). Parent UFG entities have faced franchisee criticism across brands; evaluate the parent's support model and the 35-year franchise term.
riskFlags.source: United Franchise Group press / Franchise Direct

nationwide.note: Operates ~100 locations across multiple US states plus first Canadian location (Brossard, Quebec, 2024); actively expanding into new states.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Reported total investment range $162,000-$294,000 (earlier UFG figures cited $124,884-$212,234).

franchiseFee.note: Franchise fee reported as $39,500-$49,500; lower bound used as standard new-franchisee figure.

totalUnits.note: Opened 100th location Oct 2, 2025; 32 new locations opened in 2024.

yearFranchising.note: Joined UFG family of brands in 2021.

veteranDiscount.note: US military veterans receive 20% off the initial franchise fee for their first location.

isGrowing.note: 100th location, 33% same-store sales growth in 2024, 16 consecutive positive quarters reported.
